title: Introduction to Databases
summary: Database concepts, DBMS, ACID properties, relational model, and keys.
content_html:
What is a Database?

A database is an organized collection of structured data stored electronically. A Database Management System (DBMS) is software that manages databases (MySQL, PostgreSQL, Oracle, SQLite).

Advantages over File Systems

  • Data integrity: Enforces rules (constraints) to keep data consistent.
  • Reduced redundancy: Normalization eliminates duplicate data.
  • Concurrent access: Multiple users can access data simultaneously.
  • Security: Fine-grained access control.
  • Recovery: Backup and restore capabilities.

Relational Model

Data is organized into tables (relations) with rows (tuples) and columns (attributes). All major DBMS follow this model (with SQL as the standard language).

Table: Student
+----+--------+-----+--------+
| ID | Name   | Age | Major  |
+----+--------+-----+--------+
|  1 | Alice  |  20 | CS     |
|  2 | Bob    |  22 | Math   |
+----+--------+-----+--------+

Keys

  • Primary Key (PK): Uniquely identifies each row. Cannot be NULL.
  • Foreign Key (FK): References the primary key of another table. Enforces referential integrity.
  • Candidate Key: Minimal set of columns that uniquely identifies rows.
  • Composite Key: Primary key consisting of multiple columns.

ACID Properties

Transactions in a DBMS must satisfy:

  • Atomicity: Transaction is all-or-nothing (commit or rollback).
  • Consistency: Database transitions from one valid state to another.
  • Isolation: Concurrent transactions appear to execute sequentially.
  • Durability: Committed changes persist even after crashes.

Schema vs Instance

  • Schema: The structure/design of the database (table names, column types).
  • Instance: The actual data stored at a given point in time.

Three-Tier Architecture

Application/Web Layer (presentation)
     ↓
Business Logic Layer (application)
     ↓
Database Layer (data storage)

exercises.title: Primary key
options: Uniquely identifies each row and cannot be NULL
exercises.answer: Uniquely identifies each row and cannot be NULL

exercises.title: Foreign key purpose
options: References the primary key of another table
exercises.answer: References the primary key of another table

exercises.title: Relational model organization
exercises.prompt: In the relational model, data is stored in:

exercises.title: Schema definition
options: The structure and design of the database
exercises.answer: The structure and design of the database

exercises.title: Composite key
options: A primary key made of multiple columns
A key that references another table
exercises.answer: A primary key made of multiple columns

exercises.title: ACID Isolation
options: Concurrent transactions appear sequential to each other
exercises.answer: Concurrent transactions appear sequential to each other

exercises.title: DB advantage
exercises.prompt: Compared to file systems, databases provide:
options: Better data integrity, security, and concurrent access
exercises.answer: Better data integrity, security, and concurrent access
